--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9780'
+project: local-review
+title: 'fix(review): `review working` scope must include untracked files'
+---
+## What
+
+A full `/finish` run in ../calcutron (2026-06-11) produced 8 × `review working` calls that ALL resolved to an empty scope — `review scope resolved validators=[] validator_count=0 files=0`, `attempted: 0` — even though the loop had just written an entire app (`Cargo.toml`, `src/*.rs` ×8, `tests/`). Everything was **untracked** (`??`): calcutron's HEAD is from June 4 and the new code was never committed. `review working` ("uncommitted changes vs HEAD") only picks up tracked modifications, so greenfield/new files are invisible and every review is a trivially-empty no-op that reviewer agents read as "clean".
+
+Fix the `working` scope resolution (the code that logs `review scope resolved` — follow it from the `review` MCP tool's `review working` op in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/review_op.rs` into the scope resolver in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators`):
+
+- [ ] Include untracked files (git status `??` entries, `.gitignore` respected — never `target/`, `.sah/`, etc.) in the `working` scope as whole-file review targets, the same way `review file` reviews a whole file when there is no diff.
+- [ ] An empty resolved scope must be unmistakable in the report: the markdown should say "nothing in scope to review" (and counts stay attempted=0) instead of emitting a bare findings header that reads identically to a clean review.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] In a repo with committed history plus brand-new untracked source files, `review working` resolves files > 0 and the fleet actually runs (attempted > 0).
+- [ ] Ignored files (e.g. `target/`, `.sah/`) are NOT swept into scope.
+- [ ] A genuinely clean tree (no tracked changes, no untracked files) returns a report whose markdown explicitly states nothing was in scope.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Unit/integration test in the scope-resolution module: temp git repo with a commit, add an untracked `src/new.rs` → `working` scope contains it; add a `.gitignore`d file → excluded. Fast (<10s, no model — scope resolution only).
+- [ ] Test: tracked modification + untracked file → both in scope.
+- [ ] Test: clean tree → empty scope and the "nothing in scope" markdown marker.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators -p swissarmyhammer-tools` green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
+
+## Evidence
+
+../calcutron/.sah/mcp.38775.log (2026-06-11 12:22–12:23 UTC): 8 × `tool_call args tool=review args={"op":"review working"}` → each `review scope resolved validators=[] validator_count=0 files=0`, `review verify complete candidates=0`, result `{"markdown": "## Review Findings (2026-06-11 07:22)\n", "counts": {... "attempted": 0, "failed": 0}}` in 1–16s with zero llama generation. `git status` in calcutron: every source file `??` untracked, HEAD = June 4.
\ No newline at end of file

+
+- [ ] Add `opts.recurse_untracked_dirs(true)` to `GitOperations::get_status` (crates/swissarmyhammer-git/src/operations.rs) so untracked directories expand to their contained files (`.gitignore` still respected via `include_ignored(false)`).
+- [ ] Audit other `get_status` callers for behavior change (workspace-wide grep) — the summary lists more untracked paths now.

+- [ ] An empty resolved scope must be unmistakable in the report markdown: say "nothing in scope to review" instead of a bare findings header that reads identically to a clean review (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/synthesize.rs` or wherever the markdown header is emitted).
